description: Energy action archetypes for pre-feasibility assessment. Actions are strategic interventions — upstream of site and technology selection. Each action can map to one or more technologies and financing archetypes.

actions.name: Solar PV on Public Buildings
actions.description: Install rooftop or carport solar PV on municipal buildings (schools, hospitals, admin buildings) to reduce grid electricity costs and demonstrate public commitment.
prerequisites: Building structural assessment for rooftop load
Net metering or self-consumption policy in place

emissionsReduction.basis: Grid emission factor for BR South

impactIndicators.beneficiaries: Direct: building occupants; indirect: municipal budget savings

localRelevance.portoAlegre: High — POA already procures renewable energy for 60 public facilities. Rooftop solar is a natural next step. PLAC identifies this as a priority action.

actions.name: Distribution Grid Resilience Upgrade
actions.description: Upgrade and harden distribution network to reduce outage frequency and duration, especially during extreme weather. Includes undergrounding, smart switches, vegetation management, and substation flood-proofing.

emissionsReduction.basis: Lower line losses reduce upstream generation

impactIndicators.beneficiaries: All connected households and businesses in upgraded area

supportive: Recent extreme weather events causing grid failures
Climate projections showing increased storm intensity
blocking: No regulatory framework for resilience investment recovery

localRelevance.portoAlegre: Critical — Jan 2024 storm left 1.1M without power; May 2024 floods collapsed grid in POA. Post-privatization maintenance has deteriorated. PLAC drainage works depend on reliable power for pump stations.

actions.name: Energy Efficiency Retrofit — Public Buildings
actions.description: Bundle of efficiency measures for public buildings: LED lighting, HVAC optimization, insulation, smart building controls. Quick wins with short payback.

blocking: Buildings scheduled for demolition or major renovation

localRelevance.portoAlegre: High — POA has initiated LED and retrofit projects. This action scales what's already started. Pairs naturally with solar-public-buildings.

actions.name: Electric Bus Fleet Transition
actions.description: Replace diesel buses with electric buses and deploy charging infrastructure. Addresses transport emissions (largest energy sector in RS) and air quality.

impactIndicators.airQuality: Significant PM2.5 and NOx reduction along routes
impactIndicators.beneficiaries: Bus riders, communities along bus routes

blocking: Grid cannot support depot charging load
Bus concession contract prevents fleet changes

localRelevance.portoAlegre: In progress — POA committed to 100 electric buses in PLAC. Charging infrastructure being planned. This action supports scaling beyond the initial 100.

actions.name: Community Solar + Storage for Resilience
actions.description: Deploy community-scale solar PV with battery storage in vulnerable neighborhoods to provide resilient power during grid outages and reduce energy costs for low-income households.
prerequisites: Community land or public land available

impactIndicators.resilience: 4-12 hours backup during grid outage for critical loads

supportive: Frequent grid outages in target area

localRelevance.portoAlegre: High — May 2024 floods left 138,000 without power. Pump stations failed. Community solar+storage in flood-affected neighborhoods addresses both equity and resilience.

actions.name: Biomethane from Municipal Solid Waste
actions.description: Capture landfill gas or process organic waste via anaerobic digestion to produce biomethane for vehicle fuel or grid injection.
prerequisites: Landfill with sufficient gas generation or organic waste stream
Offtake agreement (bus fleet, gas grid injection, industrial user)

emissionsReduction.range: Avoided methane emissions + fossil fuel displacement

supportive: Active landfill with >500 t/day waste input

localRelevance.portoAlegre: Active — POA joined Pacto do Biometano. Landfills in Minas do Leão and São Leopoldo already produce ~100,000 m3/day. Natural pairing with electric bus fleet transition.

actions.name: LED Street Lighting Conversion
actions.description: Convert municipal street lighting to LED with smart dimming controls. One of the fastest, most bankable municipal energy actions globally.

supportive: Existing stock is sodium/mercury vapor (>10 years old)
Public safety concerns in underlit areas
PPP framework available for ESCO model
blocking: Recently converted to LED (<5 years ago)

localRelevance.portoAlegre: Medium-High — POA has started LED initiatives. Full city-wide conversion would be a quick, visible win with strong payback. ESCO model via PPP-light is well suited.
